Acta v0.2.0 record · Generated from accepted canonical state (blueprints/initiative-slug.md) after explicit acceptance (chat — Gate B confirmed; Gate A previously accepted) · Never generated from browser state.

decision record · ACCEPTED

Accepted change blueprint

One paragraph: what was specified, how it will be built, and why both gates closed.

Accepted gates

Gate A — specification

One-paragraph restatement of the accepted scope and non-goals.

Rationale: The accepted spec rationale.. Accepted via chat — explicit confirmation.

Gate B — implementation plan

approve-plan — Approve this plan as the build contract?

Rationale: The accepted plan rationale.. Accepted via chat — explicit confirmation.

Slice plan

Vertical slices; every slice leaves the tests green.

SliceDeliversTouches
Slice 1First independently green slice

Slice 3User-visible wiring

Data flow

State the dependency direction the plan preserves.

Explain what solid and dashed edges mean here.

Riskiest code surface

The single trickiest surface in the plan, shown as real code.

path/planned-surface.mjs (planned)
export function plannedSurface(input) {
  // the invariant this code must hold
  return transform(input);
}

Risks, tests, rollback

RiskLikelihoodMitigation
A real failure mode of this planmediumHow a slice or test bounds it.

Proof

  • what proves the core
  • what proves the wiring

Rollout, then rollback

  • how the slices reach users safely
Rollback
  • the single step that undoes the change

Unresolved at acceptance

  • anything decided during the build

Sources

  1. [E1] spec.md — accepted at Gate A

Acta is a print-first engineering record. Fact = directly supported; Inference = reasoned from evidence; Simplification = deliberately reduced model. This record renders accepted canonical state only; open decisions live in the companion instrument.
